facts.statement: TJX describes itself as the leading off-price retailer of apparel and home fashions in the U.S. and worldwide.
At the end of fiscal 2026, TJX said it operated over 5,200 stores across its four segments.
TJX said its four segments are Marmaxx, HomeGoods, TJX Canada, and TJX International; it also said Sierra operates retail stores and sierra.com in the U.S.

Marmaxx operates TJ Maxx, Marshalls, tjmaxx.com, and marshalls.com; HomeGoods operates HomeGoods and Homesense; TJX Canada operates Winners, HomeSense, and Marshalls in Canada; TJX International operates TK Maxx, Homesense, tkmaxx.com, tkmaxx.de, and tkmaxx.at in Europe, and TK Maxx in Australia.
TJX said it offers brand-name and designer merchandise at prices generally 20% to 60% below full-price retailers' regular prices on comparable merchandise.
TJX said its merchandise mix includes good, better, and best merchandise intended to reach a broad range of consumers.
TJX said its stores use a treasure-hunt shopping experience and that fresh merchandise flows into stores regularly to encourage frequent customer visits and cross-shopping across banners.
TJX said it sources merchandise opportunistically, including from department store cancellations, manufacturer overruns, and closeouts from brands and other retailers.
TJX said it also develops some merchandise itself to supplement or fill gaps in its expected assortment.
TJX said its logistics and distribution operations are designed to support quick, efficient, and differentiated delivery across its geographically diverse store network.
TJX said its stores have adaptable layouts without walls between departments and are largely free of permanent fixtures so they can be adjusted to changing merchandise mixes.
TJX said it tailors merchandise to local preferences and customer demographics and seeks rapid inventory turnover across a wide array of products.
TJX said it had approximately 377,000 employees at the end of fiscal 2026, including full-time, part-time, temporary, and seasonal Associates.
TJX reported net sales of $60.4 billion for fiscal 2026 and said annual sales surpassed $60 billion for the first time.
TJX said consolidated comparable sales grew 5% in fiscal 2026, with strong comparable sales increases at each division.
SEC company facts show TJX reported net income of $4.474 billion and operating cash flow of $6.057 billion for FY2026.
SEC company facts show TJX ended FY2026 with $5.335 billion of cash and equivalents, $6.364 billion of stockholders' equity, and $2.866 billion of long-term debt.
TJX said it returned $4.3 billion to shareholders in fiscal 2026.
TJX said it added 129 net new stores and remodeled almost 500 stores during fiscal 2026.
TJX said its global store base spanned three continents and that it ended fiscal 2026 with stores in 9 countries.
The proxy letter was signed by Carol Meyrowitz as Executive Chairman of the Board and Ernie Herrman as Chief Executive Officer and President.
TJX said ten directors were nominated for election at the 2026 Annual Meeting, with the nominees to serve until the 2027 Annual Meeting if elected.
TJX asked shareholders to ratify PricewaterhouseCoopers as its independent registered public accounting firm for fiscal 2027.

TJX's proxy statement identified risks including buying strategy and inventory management, competition, merchandise sourcing and transport, international trade and tariff policies, data security and IT systems, labor costs and workforce challenges, currency exchange rates, compliance, litigation, tax matters, and cash flow and long-term indebtedness.

TJX said virtually all of its store locations, plus some distribution and fulfillment centers and office space, are under operating leases; it reported total operating lease liabilities of $10.620 billion as of January 31, 2026.
TJX said it had five series of senior unsecured notes outstanding as of January 31, 2026, with maturities in 2026, 2028, 2030, 2031, and 2050, and total debt of $2.878 billion.
TJX said it had two revolving credit facilities totaling $1.5 billion of borrowing capacity as of January 31, 2026, with no amounts outstanding and compliance with all related covenants.
TJX said its worldwide effective income tax rate for fiscal 2026 was 24.7% and that it paid $1.471 billion of income taxes in cash during the year.
